project_goal: AIOS — AI-native Linux distribution. Implement the spec contracts from rev.2 (53 sub-specs CONTRACT-grade) into working Rust + Python + KDE code, following §22 MVP golden path discipline.
current_milestone: M1 — Action Envelope skeleton (S0.1 implementation, foundation for all action dispatch)
current_step: T-005 complete; T-006 queued — integration tests under tests/, golden JSON fixtures for representative envelope shapes (success path, policy denial, rollback), JCS canonical-encoding round-trip golden tests, and possibly adding a vetted RFC 8785 ECMA-262 number-normalization dep (governor approval required)
completed_steps: Workspace root Cargo.toml created with §21 stack lock pins (serde, tokio-ready, tonic-ready, tracing)
T-001: aios-action crate skeleton + 8 prefix-namespaced ID types + 5-phase FSM + 4-section envelope + 11-variant error subset; 10 unit tests; cargo build/test/clippy all pass

T-002: canonical.rs (jcs_canonicalize + blake3_hash + blake3_truncated + CanonicalError); Request::request_hash; ActionEnvelope::idempotency_hash; 14 new tests (10 → 24); no new deps added; ECMA-262 number normalization deferred to T-006 with documented limitation

T-003: extended prefix-namespace registry — 5 ULID-bodied newtypes (apprq_, appb_, ovrq_, ovr_, actrq_) reuse define_prefixed_id macro; TransitionPlanId (tplan_) is content-addressed with from_content(bytes)/parse(s) and intentionally exposes NO random new() constructor (S15.2 §5.3 byte-identical-replay invariant); IdError::InvalidHexBody added; 8 new tests (24 → 32); clippy clean after splitting macro doc first paragraphs and renaming single-char test bindings

T-004: lifecycle FSM behaviour layer — ConditionType closed enum (13 variants per S0.1 §5.3); Condition.condition_type refactor (was String); ActionEnvelope::transition_to enforces S0.1 §6.2 six edges + §6.3 terminality + §6.7 phase_changed_at monotonicity; ActionEnvelope::add_condition enforces append-only conditions list (no timestamp regression, no True→False flip on same condition_type); canonical_conditions_for_phase mirrors §6.6 mapping; validate_phase_conditions checks the actual list against the canonical required set; TransitionError enum (IllegalTransition/TerminalPhase/MonotonicityViolation/PhaseConditionMismatch) in error.rs; 25 new tests (32 → 57); full JSON round-trip with replay validation green; clippy clean

T-005: full error taxonomy — ActionErrorCode closed enum (35 variants per S0.1 §7.3 + §13.3: 11 validation + 5 policy + 3 authorization + 8 execution + 3 verification + 2 rollback + 3 infrastructure); replaced the 11-variant T-001 thiserror ActionError enum with the canonical envelope-shaped struct (code/message/retryable/cause); ActionErrorCode::retryable_default helper returns Some(false)/Some(true)/None per §7.4, with the two 'depends' codes (AdapterFailure, Internal) returning None; cause-chain bounded at MAX_CAUSE_CHAIN_DEPTH=8 via with_cause -> Result<Self, CauseChainTooDeep>; CauseChainTooDeep is a separate programmer-facing error (NOT an ActionErrorCode variant); From<IdError> -> EnvelopeMalformed/retryable=false; From<TransitionError> -> Internal/retryable=false; serde round-trip of §7.5 three-deep cause-chain example matches verbatim; 19 new tests (57 → 76); clippy clean (added scoped #[allow(clippy::match_same_arms)] on retryable_default because the spec §7.3 grouping is load-bearing)
remaining_steps_M1: T-006: aios-action integration tests + golden fixtures (and approved RFC 8785 dep for full JCS number normalization)
discovered_constraints: Workspace lints set: unsafe_code forbid, unwrap/expect/panic deny, clippy::pedantic warn
§21 stack lock: serde + tokio + tonic + serde + tracing pinned at workspace level
chrono added to workspace deps (needed by S0.1 §5.1 Condition.last_transition_time and Execution timestamps)
thiserror auto-source: a field named `source: String` collides with the AsDynError trait — use `detail: String` for stringified non-error contexts
Tests need `#[allow(clippy::expect_used)]` since workspace denies expect/unwrap globally — applied at test mod level

serde_json::Map defaults to BTreeMap (preserve_order feature disabled at workspace level), giving lex-sorted keys for free — but we still rebuild maps explicitly during canonicalization to defend against future workspace config drift

Full RFC 8785 ECMA-262 number formatting NOT implemented (serde_json default used); fine for Request's current surface (target: Value rarely contains floats outside integers); golden-fixture work in T-006 will need a vetted serde_jcs-equivalent dep added with Governor approval
clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph fires when a macro-emitted doc comment's first paragraph spans multiple sentences; split into one-sentence summary + blank doc line + body
clippy::many_single_char_names fires at 5+ single-char locals in one scope (multi-namespace round-trip tests) — use descriptive names like apprq/appb/ovrq/ovr/actrq
TransitionPlanId has NO random new() constructor by design — content addressing is the invariant (S15.2 §5.3 invariant 5: byte-identical replay); adding new() would silently violate this
clippy::doc_markdown flags bare PascalCase identifiers in docs — wrap policy/condition vocabulary words like Allow/Deny/RequireApproval/PascalCase/RolledBack in backticks
clippy::panic denies panic! in non-test code; for tests, extend the test mod's #[allow(...)] to include clippy::panic when destructuring assertions need a panic-on-mismatch arm
Execution::pending() was `const fn` in T-001 but had to drop `const` in T-004 because chrono::Utc::now() is not const — initial phase_changed_at stamp must be a wall-clock now

clippy::match_same_arms fires when arms with the same body are not consolidated; when the spec's §7.3 grouping is load-bearing for readability (validation/policy/auth/execution/verification/rollback/infra), scope an #[allow(...)] to the single function rather than collapsing across spec groups

clippy::redundant_clone in tests requires either consuming the value (`let _ = x.into()`) or capturing what you need *before* the consuming conversion (e.g. `let msg = x.to_string(); let e: ActionError = x.into();`)
Inside a struct impl, `Option<Box<Self>>` is preferred over `Option<Box<SelfTypeName>>` (clippy::use_self)
discovered_dependencies: chrono 0.4 (std + clock + serde features, default-features = false)
last_verification_result: T-005: cargo build --workspace PASS; cargo test --workspace PASS (76/76, +19 new); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings PASS
